# Research - 2026-07-21

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins)

> Tuesday, `date -u +%u` = 2. Weekly repos (claude-code, aider) skipped per
> cadence rule. Cline shipped two HIGH-value features: optional provider
> peer dependencies (#12379: saves ~355MB install size by making
> claude-agent-sdk and @openai/codex optional, loads via dynamic import) and
> OAuth token refresh for delegated agents (#12369: teammates/subagents now
> refresh expired tokens mid-turn, retrying once after refresh). Also
> shipped MODERATE shell resolution rationalization (#12331: ensures shell
> description matches execution on Windows, probes all pwsh locations,
> resolves array-valued terminal profiles). Kilocode shows IDE-specific
> features (skills settings in JetBrains, context parity, file search
> migration) and UI polish (diff bleeding fix, branch label sync). OpenCode
> shows UI improvements (mention handling, theme sync, review panel) and
> provider-specific fixes (Mistral tool call ID normalization). Zero open
> `auto-implement` issues (unchanged). Two actionable features: #1 (optional
> provider deps, HIGH, ~6h) is highly portable; #2 (OAuth refresh, HIGH,
> ~5h) requires evaluating Alexi's delegated-agent credential flow (Alexi
> has auth error taxonomy in `src/providers/auth.ts` but no OAuth
> implementation, uses static env vars).

## Executive Summary

- **#1 (HIGH, ~6h, deps)**: Cline #12379 made large provider binaries
  optional peerDependencies instead of hard dependencies.
  `ai-sdk-provider-claude-code` (~250MB claude-agent-sdk native binary) and
  `ai-sdk-provider-codex-cli` (~105MB @openai/codex) were pulled on every
  `npm install` even for users who never select those providers. The PR
  moves both to optional peerDependencies (kept as devDependencies for
  monorepo builds) and loads via literal dynamic imports, mirroring the
  existing opencode-sdk pattern. The Claude Code provider now resolves the
  claude executable explicitly (bundled platform package when present,
  otherwise user-installed `claude` from PATH), bypassing the agent SDK's
  own resolution which fails from Bun-compiled binaries (bunfs virtual
  paths). Codex already degrades gracefully (npx -y @openai/codex, then
  PATH). Port to Alexi: Alexi uses SAP AI Core (single provider
  architecture via `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts`), so the exact
  use case (multiple heavy provider binaries) does not apply. The principle
  (large optional dependencies loaded dynamically) is portable if Alexi
  adds more providers in the future. Check `package.json` for any large
  dependencies that are provider-specific or feature-gated (e.g. MorphSDK
  for warpgrep, tree-sitter grammars for definitions). If found, move to
  optional peerDependencies and load via dynamic import with graceful
  fallback. HIGH value (reduces install size and startup time; users only
  pay for what they use; improves onboarding). Estimated ~6h: audit
  `package.json` for large/optional deps (~1h), refactor provider loading
  to dynamic imports (~3h: identify load sites, add try/catch, surface
  'provider not installed' errors), test with and without optional deps
  (~1.5h), update docs (~30m).
- **#2 (HIGH, ~5h, provider)**: Cline #12369 added OAuth token refresh for
  delegated agents (teammates, subagents). Teammate and subagent sessions
  inherit the OAuth access token as a snapshot at spawn time and had no
  refresh path: when the token expired while the lead was blocked (e.g. in
  team_await_runs), their next model call died with the provider's raw 401
  body. Only the lead's turn-start sync and runWithAuthRetry could refresh,
  and neither runs mid-turn. The PR adds an onAuthError hook to AgentConfig,
  wired once per session by LocalRuntimeHost: it refreshes credentials
  through the shared single-flight RuntimeOAuthTokenManager and propagates
  the new key to the lead, delegated defaults, and all teammates via the
  existing updateConnection channels. SessionRuntime retries a run once when
  it failed with an auth-like error and the refresh succeeded, continuing
  from the persisted trail so completed iterations aren't replayed. Also
  fixes isLikelyAuthError to lowercase string inputs. Port to Alexi: Alexi
  has delegated agents via `src/tool/tools/task.ts` (task tool spawns
  subagents) and auth error taxonomy in `src/providers/auth.ts`
  (InvalidCredentialsError, TokenExpiredError), but no OAuth flow
  implementation (uses static env vars AICORE_SERVICE_KEY, SAP_PROXY_*). The
  principle (detect auth errors mid-turn, refresh credentials, retry once)
  is portable if Alexi adds OAuth or token-based auth in the future. Review
  `src/providers/auth.ts` and `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts` to see if
  SAP AI Core tokens can expire mid-session. If yes, implement token refresh
  logic following Cline's pattern: detect 401/TokenExpired, refresh via
  provider SDK, propagate to subagents, retry once. If no (SAP tokens are
  long-lived or session-bound), document the credential lifetime contract.
  HIGH value (prevents cryptic 401 errors mid-task; improves multi-agent
  reliability; users no longer need to restart sessions when tokens expire).
  Estimated ~5h: review SAP AI Core token lifecycle (~1h), implement refresh
  hook if applicable (~2h: detect auth errors, refresh via SAP SDK,
  propagate to subagents), add retry logic (~1h), test with expired token
  (~1h).
- **#3 (MODERATE, ~4h, tool)**: Cline #12331 rationalized shell
  identification and prompting on Windows. The default-shell fallback only
  checked the Program Files pwsh path, so Microsoft Store installs of
  PowerShell 7 fell back to Windows PowerShell while VS Code's own terminal
  launched pwsh. The PR shares one candidate list between the sync
  default-shell check and the async PowerShell prober, resolves array-valued
  terminal profile paths on all platforms (not just Windows), and applies
  terminal profile changes at the model-request boundary (the
  run_commands tool snapshots {profileId, shell} in a provider function;
  both execution paths consume the snapshot, so commands produced by an
  in-flight inference run with the shell the model was told about). Port to
  Alexi: Alexi has `src/tool/tools/bash.ts` and `src/tool/tools/shell.ts`
  for shell execution. Review to see if shell detection and description
  match execution. Check if the bash tool description is static or
  dynamically generated per-request (Cline's fix ensures the description
  names the correct shell at request time). If the description is static and
  shell is detected once at startup, consider making it dynamic or
  re-detecting on profile changes. MODERATE value (prevents
  description/execution mismatches; improves Windows PowerShell detection;
  edge case on other platforms). Estimated ~4h: review bash tool
  implementation (~1h), refactor shell detection if static (~1.5h: probe
  candidate shells, resolve array-valued paths, snapshot per-request), test
  on Windows/macOS/Linux (~1h), update docs (~30m).
- **#4 (SKIP, insufficient_credits)**: Cline #12394 detects
  `insufficient_credits` error from Cline API. Alexi uses SAP AI Core, not
  Cline API. Skip.
- **#5 (SKIP, .clineignore deprecation)**: Cline #12410 documents that
  `.clineignore` is deprecated soon because enforcement is extremely
  difficult (agents can get around ignore rules via @ mentions or shell
  commands). Alexi does not have a `.clineignore` feature. Skip (no action
  needed; if Alexi adds ignore rules in the future, note this pitfall).
- **Rotation**: kilocode 3 dryness (IDE-specific features and UI polish, no
  portable items; 3 < 5, keep daily), cline 0 dryness (active, three items
  analyzed: optional peer deps, OAuth refresh, shell resolution), opencode 3
  dryness (UI improvements and provider-specific fixes, no portable items; 3
  < 5, keep daily). No demotions.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. Optional provider peer dependencies (deps, ~6h, HIGH)

- **What**: Cline #12379 made large provider binaries optional
  peerDependencies instead of hard dependencies. `ai-sdk-provider-claude-code`
  (~250MB claude-agent-sdk platform binary) and
  `ai-sdk-provider-codex-cli` (~105MB @openai/codex) were pulled on every
  `npm install` even for users who never select those providers. The PR
  moves both to optional peerDependencies (kept as devDependencies so
  monorepo builds still bundle the JS) and loads them via literal dynamic
  imports in `community.ts`, mirroring the existing opencode-sdk pattern.
  The Claude Code provider now resolves the claude executable explicitly:
  bundled platform package when present, otherwise a user-installed `claude`
  from PATH, passed via `defaultSettings.pathToClaudeCodeExecutable`. The
  agent SDK's own resolution cannot be used from Bun-compiled binaries
  because it anchors on the virtual bunfs where node_modules lookups never
  see packages on disk. Codex already degrades gracefully (npx -y
  @openai/codex, then codex on PATH). The PR saves ~355MB on install for
  users who do not use those providers. Part of Cline's multi-provider
  architecture (supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode,
  Bedrock, Vertex, and more).
- **Why**: Alexi uses SAP AI Core (single provider architecture via
  `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts`), so the exact use case (multiple
  heavy provider binaries) does not apply. Alexi routes to different models
  via `routing-config.json` but all models are accessed through the same SAP
  Orchestration SDK. The principle (large optional dependencies loaded
  dynamically) is portable if Alexi adds more providers in the future
  (e.g. fallback to direct Anthropic API, local ollama, etc.). Port to
  Alexi: audit `package.json` for any large dependencies that are
  provider-specific or feature-gated. Candidates: (1) MorphSDK for warpgrep
  (`src/tool/tools/warpgrep.ts` uses semantic code search via MorphSDK —
  check if this is a heavy dependency and if it is always needed); (2)
  tree-sitter grammars for definitions tool (`src/tool/tools/definitions.ts`
  uses tree-sitter for symbol extraction — check if grammars are bundled or
  optional); (3) any other native binaries or large libraries. If found,
  move to optional peerDependencies and load via dynamic import
  (`await import('...')` in a try/catch, surface 'dependency not installed'
  error with npm install command). HIGH value (reduces install size and
  startup time; users only pay for what they use; improves onboarding;
  aligns with modern dependency hygiene). Estimated ~6h: audit
  `package.json` for large/optional deps (~1h: check installed sizes via
  `npm list --depth=0`, identify feature-gated deps), refactor provider
  loading to dynamic imports (~3h: identify load sites, add try/catch with
  clear error messages, surface 'provider not installed' to user), test with
  and without optional deps (~1.5h: fresh install without deps, verify error
  messages, install deps and verify feature works), update docs (~30m:
  document optional deps and how to install).
- **Source**: cline/cline PR #12379 "feat(llms): make Claude Code and Codex
  provider packages optional peers" (merged 2026-07-21T05:27Z, commit
  `8a9e1f3`). Full PR body at https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12379.
- **Category**: deps. **Size**: moderate (~6h: audit package.json, refactor
  to dynamic imports, test with/without deps, update docs).
- **Status**: HIGH (reduces install size by ~355MB equivalent if Alexi has
  similar large deps; improves onboarding; portable to any feature-gated
  dependency; aligns with Cline's approach).

#### 2. OAuth token refresh for delegated agents (provider, ~5h, HIGH)

- **What**: Cline #12369 added OAuth token refresh for delegated agents
  (teammates, subagents). Teammate and subagent sessions inherit the OAuth
  access token as a snapshot at spawn time and had no refresh path: when the
  token expired while the lead was blocked (e.g. in team_await_runs), their
  next model call died with the provider's raw 401 body. Only the lead's
  turn-start sync and runWithAuthRetry could refresh, and neither runs
  mid-turn. The PR adds an onAuthError hook to AgentConfig, wired once per
  session by LocalRuntimeHost: it refreshes credentials through the shared
  single-flight RuntimeOAuthTokenManager and propagates the new key to the
  lead, delegated defaults, and all teammates via the existing
  updateConnection channels. SessionRuntime retries a run once when it
  failed with an auth-like error and the refresh succeeded, continuing from
  the persisted trail so completed iterations aren't replayed. Also fixes
  isLikelyAuthError to lowercase string inputs (the server's 'Unauthorized:
  ...' message only matched when wrapped in an Error). Part of Cline's OAuth
  flow for ClinePass / cloud API access.
- **Why**: Alexi has delegated agents via `src/tool/tools/task.ts` (task
  tool spawns subagents with `explore` or `general` types) and auth error
  taxonomy in `src/providers/auth.ts` (InvalidCredentialsError,
  TokenExpiredError, MissingCredentialsError, NetworkError, RateLimitError).
  However, Alexi does not implement OAuth: it uses static environment
  variables (AICORE_SERVICE_KEY, AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP,
  AICORE_DEPLOYMENT_ID, SAP_PROXY_*) loaded at startup via dotenv (see
  `.env.example`). The principle (detect auth errors mid-turn, refresh
  credentials, retry once) is portable if Alexi adds OAuth or token-based
  auth in the future. Port to Alexi: (1) Review SAP AI Core token lifecycle.
  Check if `AICORE_SERVICE_KEY` is a long-lived credential (like a service
  account key) or a short-lived OAuth token. Read SAP AI Core SDK docs
  (`@sap-ai-sdk/orchestration`, used in `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts`)
  to see if tokens can expire mid-session and if the SDK supports token
  refresh. (2) If SAP AI Core uses long-lived keys (no expiry), document the
  credential lifetime contract and skip this feature (no action needed). (3)
  If SAP AI Core uses short-lived tokens that can expire, implement token
  refresh logic following Cline's pattern: detect 401/TokenExpired errors in
  `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts`, refresh via SAP SDK or OAuth flow,
  propagate refreshed credentials to subagents spawned by task tool, retry
  once. HIGH value (prevents cryptic 401 errors mid-task; improves
  multi-agent reliability; users no longer need to restart sessions when
  tokens expire; critical for long-running agent workflows). Estimated ~5h:
  review SAP AI Core token lifecycle (~1h: read SAP SDK docs, check if
  AICORE_SERVICE_KEY can expire, identify refresh API), implement refresh
  hook if applicable (~2h: add onAuthError callback to provider, refresh via
  SAP SDK, propagate to task tool subagents via existing session manager),
  add retry logic (~1h: detect auth errors, retry once after refresh,
  continue from persisted trail), test with expired token (~1h: mock expired
  token, verify refresh and retry, test subagent propagation).
- **Source**: cline/cline PR #12369 "fix(sdk): retry runs once after
  refreshing expired OAuth credentials" (merged 2026-07-21T04:58Z, commit
  `7b8c9e2`). Full PR body at https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12369.
  Related: #12370 "fix(sdk): report errored teammate runs as failed instead
  of completed" (ensures runs with finishReason 'error' report status
  'failed' so retry machinery engages).
- **Category**: provider. **Size**: moderate (~5h: review SAP token
  lifecycle, implement refresh hook if needed, add retry logic, test).
- **Status**: HIGH (prevents mid-turn auth failures; improves multi-agent
  reliability; critical for long-running workflows; conditional on SAP AI
  Core token lifecycle — confirm tokens can expire before implementing).

#### 3. Rationalize shell resolution to match description with execution (tool, ~4h, MODERATE)

- **What**: Cline #12331 rationalized shell identification and prompting,
  especially on Windows. Previously the default-shell fallback only checked
  the Program Files pwsh path, so Microsoft Store installs of PowerShell 7
  fell back to Windows PowerShell while VS Code's own terminal launched
  pwsh, causing a mismatch between the shell described in the run_commands
  tool prompt and the shell actually executing commands. The PR (1) shares
  one candidate list between the sync default-shell check and the async
  PowerShell prober, probing all pwsh install locations (Program Files,
  Program Files (x86), Local AppData); (2) resolves array-valued terminal
  profile paths on all platforms (not just Windows) — VS Code permits
  'path' to be string | string[] on every platform, but the resolver crashed
  for array values on macOS/Linux; (3) applies terminal profile changes at
  the model-request boundary instead of triggering a deferred session
  rebuild — the run_commands tool snapshots {profileId, shell} in a provider
  function that re-derives the description each time the runtime reads it,
  so the description names the shell that will actually execute. Part of
  Cline's shell tool (`run_commands`) which generates a shell-specific
  prompt (PowerShell syntax vs bash syntax).
- **Why**: Alexi has `src/tool/tools/bash.ts` and `src/tool/tools/shell.ts`
  for shell execution. Review to see if (1) shell detection and description
  match execution, (2) the bash tool description is static or dynamically
  generated per-request (Cline's fix ensures the description names the
  correct shell at request time), (3) shell is detected once at startup or
  re-detected on profile changes. Port to Alexi: If the bash tool
  description is static and shell is detected once at startup, consider
  making it dynamic (regenerate description at request time, snapshot shell
  per-request) or re-detecting on profile changes. Check if Alexi supports
  multiple shell profiles or if it always uses the system default shell. If
  Alexi generates shell-specific syntax hints (PowerShell vs bash vs zsh),
  ensure the hint matches the actual shell. MODERATE value (prevents
  description/execution mismatches; improves Windows PowerShell detection;
  edge case on macOS/Linux where array-valued paths are rare). Estimated
  ~4h: review bash tool implementation (~1h: check how shell is detected,
  whether description is static or dynamic, if profile changes are
  supported), refactor shell detection if static (~1.5h: probe candidate
  shells on Windows/macOS/Linux, resolve array-valued paths, snapshot
  per-request), test on Windows/macOS/Linux (~1h: verify description matches
  execution, test profile changes), update docs (~30m: document shell
  detection logic).
- **Source**: cline/cline PR #12331 "fix(vscode): shell mismatch between
  prompt, execution, and user configuration on Windows" (merged
  2026-07-21T03:47Z, commit `9c8d1f4`). Full PR body at
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12331.
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: moderate (~4h: review bash tool, refactor
  shell detection if static, test on multiple platforms, update docs).
- **Status**: MODERATE (prevents description/execution mismatches; improves
  Windows shell detection; edge case on other platforms; conditional on
  Alexi's current shell detection approach — confirm static vs dynamic
  before implementing).

#### 4. Other non-portable / low-signal commits

- Cline #12410 "docs: mark .clineignore as deprecated soon": Documents that
  `.clineignore` enforcement is extremely difficult (agents can bypass via @
  mentions or shell commands) and the feature is orphaned in extensions.
  Alexi does not have `.clineignore`. Skip (if Alexi adds ignore rules in
  the future, note this pitfall and consider alternative approaches like
  permission prompts per-access).
- Cline #12416 "feat(desktop): log telemetry events in desktop app":
  Desktop-specific telemetry. Alexi has telemetry infrastructure in
  `src/utils/telemetry.ts` but it is not production-connected ("For now, we
  just store locally"). Skip (telemetry is already in place; connecting to
  an external service is a product decision, not a technical feature to
  port).
- Cline #12415 "feat(desktop): tool use group", #12414 "improve session
  overviews", #12411 "diff view layout", #12348 "account context and window
  title utilities", #11851 "task lifecycle telemetry", #12394 "detect
  insufficient_credits from Cline API", #12380 "add Kimi K3 to ClinePass
  docs", #12393 "add Kimi K3 to ClinePass model list", #12392 "add Kimi K3
  to bundled model fallback", #12379 already analyzed, #12371 "stop
  exposing team spawn tool to teammates", #12370 already mentioned with
  #12369, #12369 already analyzed, #12331 already analyzed, #12374 "agent
  chat UI components, Storybook, npm releases": Desktop UI (tool grouping,
  session overviews, diff layout, account context, chat components),
  telemetry, Cline API credit detection (not applicable to Alexi),
  model-specific updates (Kimi K3 for ClinePass, not applicable to SAP AI
  Core), lead-only tool enforcement (Alexi's task tool already spawns
  subagents, check if teammates can spawn sub-subagents — if not, skip). Low
  signal (desktop app UI, Cline API specifics, model catalog updates).
- Kilocode #12416 "feat(jetbrains): add skills settings", #12335
  "fix(cli): avoid standalone TUI preload lookup", #12314 "fix: keep CLI
  sidebar branch label in sync with git", #12191 "feat(jetbrains): migration
  from JetBrains file search to CLI", #12292 "feat(jetbrains): add Context
  settings parity", #12415 "fix(ui): stop diff content bleeding through
  sticky tool card headers", skills editor syntax, run file mention searches
  concurrently, improve skills settings, treat remote skills as read-only:
  JetBrains IDE plugin features (skills UI, context settings, file search

  exists in `src/skill/`; UI polish not applicable).
- OpenCode #37986 "generalize session title model description", #37982
  "normalize Mistral tool call IDs", #37562 "update muse-spark reasoning
  default and system prompt", #36258 "display proper session import errors",
  "remove desktop promo overlay", #37941 "complete mentions at cursor",
  #37940 "preserve mentions during paste", #37942 "preserve command menu
  drafts", #37931 "sync embedded terminal theme", #37923 "bump ghostty-web",
  #37918 "tolerate missing message parents", #37909 "shrink Windows drawer
  close button", #37895 "align Windows upgrade drawer", #37890 "gate
  notification server selection", #37577 "omit empty prompt text parts",
  #36716 "review panel improvements": Desktop app UI (mention handling,
  theme sync, terminal rendering, drawer sizing, review panel),
  provider-specific fixes (Mistral tool call ID normalization, muse-spark
  reasoning), session import error display, prompt text cleanup. Low signal
  (desktop app UI, model-specific tuning, error messages — none directly
  portable to Alexi CLI).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Priority order**: Two HIGH items (#1 optional peer deps, #2 OAuth
   refresh), one MODERATE item (#3 shell resolution), two SKIP items (#4
   insufficient_credits, #5 .clineignore). #1 is the highest-value item
   today: reduces install size by auditing for large optional deps and
   loading dynamically (portable to any feature-gated dependency). #2 is
   HIGH but conditional: requires confirming SAP AI Core tokens can expire
   mid-session. #3 is MODERATE: improves shell detection accuracy but is an
   edge case.
2. **Optional peer deps (#1)**: Surface as an issue after auditing
   `package.json` for large dependencies. Check (1) MorphSDK for warpgrep
   (is it always needed? can it be optional?), (2) tree-sitter grammars for
   definitions tool (bundled or optional?), (3) any other native binaries or
   large libraries. If found, follow Cline's pattern: move to optional
   peerDependencies, load via dynamic import with try/catch, surface clear
   error message with npm install command. Reference: Cline #12379
   (https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12379). Estimated ~6h: audit
   package.json (~1h), refactor to dynamic imports (~3h), test (~1.5h),
   update docs (~30m). HIGH value.
3. **OAuth refresh (#2)**: Do NOT surface as an issue until SAP AI Core
   token lifecycle is confirmed. Action: Read SAP AI Core SDK docs
   (`@sap-ai-sdk/orchestration`) to see if AICORE_SERVICE_KEY is a
   long-lived credential or a short-lived token that can expire mid-session.
   If long-lived, skip. If short-lived, implement token refresh following
   Cline's pattern: detect 401/TokenExpired in
   `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts`, refresh via SAP SDK, propagate to
   subagents, retry once. Reference: Cline #12369
   (https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12369). Estimated ~5h if needed.
   HIGH value (conditional).
4. **Shell resolution (#3)**: Do NOT surface as an issue until Alexi's
   current shell detection is reviewed. Action: Read
   `src/tool/tools/bash.ts` and `src/tool/tools/shell.ts` to see if (1)
   shell detection is static or dynamic, (2) description is regenerated
   per-request or once at startup, (3) profile changes are supported. If
   description is static and does not match execution, refactor following
   Cline's pattern: probe candidate shells, resolve array-valued paths,
   snapshot per-request. Reference: Cline #12331
   (https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12331). Estimated ~4h if needed.
   MODERATE value.
5. **Insufficient credits (#4)**: Do NOT surface as an issue. Cline #12394
   detects `insufficient_credits` from Cline API. Alexi uses SAP AI Core,
   not Cline API. Skip.
6. **.clineignore deprecation (#5)**: Do NOT surface as an issue. Cline
   #12410 documents that ignore rule enforcement is difficult. Alexi does
   not have `.clineignore`. Note for future: if Alexi adds ignore rules,
   consider permission prompts per-access instead of hard enforcement
   (agents can bypass via @ mentions or shell commands). Skip.
7. **Rotation**: kilocode 3 dryness (IDE-specific features, UI polish, no
   portable items; 3 < 5, keep daily), cline 0 dryness (active, three items
   analyzed: optional peer deps, OAuth refresh, shell resolution), opencode
   3 dryness (UI improvements, provider-specific fixes, no portable items; 3
   < 5, keep daily). Next weekly poll: Monday 2026-07-27. No demotions.
8. **Observation**: Zero open `auto-implement` issues (unchanged). The
   engineering pipeline remains clear. Today's scan produced two HIGH items
   (#1 optional peer deps is immediately actionable via package.json audit;
   #2 OAuth refresh is conditional on SAP token lifecycle) and one MODERATE
   item (#3 shell resolution, conditional on current bash tool
   implementation). Agent 2 should surface #1 after auditing package.json
   and evaluating #2 and #3 by reading the relevant source files.
9. **Carry-forward from yesterday (2026-07-20)**: Yesterday's brief
   identified one MODERATE item (#1 bash tree-sitter tags, conditional on
   Alexi's repomap/definitions implementation) and two LOW items (#2
   expanded ANTHROPIC_MODELS, #3 context overflow patterns, both
   conditional). The day before (2026-07-19) surfaced zero actionable
   issues. 2026-07-18 identified two items: #1 custom file extensions
   (MODERATE, ~3h) and #3 sidebar usage (LOW, ~2h). 2026-07-17 identified
   five items: #1 remote slash commands (HIGH, ~5h), #2 auto-CA harvesting
   (HIGH, ~6h), #3 UTF-8 BOM frontmatter (MODERATE, ~3h), #4 max output
   token handling (MODERATE, ~4h), #5 document subagent depth (LOW, ~1h).
   Total carry-forward: 10 items (5 from 2026-07-17 + 2 from 2026-07-18 + 0
   from 2026-07-19 + 3 from 2026-07-20). Today adds 2 HIGH (1 immediate, 1
   conditional) + 1 MODERATE (conditional) = up to 13 items in the pipeline
   if all conditionals are applicable.
